mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals rising aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in > 50% of months with rising PAYEMS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals a zero-lower-bound monetary regime that suppresses risk-free rates and drives capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based demand expansion that outpaces supply constraints, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both series to move together; neither alone is sufficient.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS does not improve the prediction of 20-day returns compared to a model with only main effects (likelihood ratio test p > 0.05), or returns are negative when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals that economic slack is being absorbed faster than expected, lifting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, a decline in UNRATE is followed by negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the strategy underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 200bps annually.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above the 97.0 index level signals that manufacturing activity has normalized past a post-recession threshold, reducing downside earnings risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO crosses above 97.0, 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of events over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and the CPI level is above 225, negative real interest rates drive capital out of fixed income and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This effect depends on the joint presence of both conditions.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional returns (Welch t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term coefficient is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 700k units signal a recovering residential construction sector that multiplies into broader demand for durable goods and employment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 700, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of observations, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 135000k indicate a tight labor market where wage pressure compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS > 135000, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the negative return prediction fails to outperform a random walk (DM test p > 0.05).
